text.title: Jenkins Shared Libraries
text.summary: Shared library structure, function patterns, and versioning to centralize reusable pipeline logic across Jenkinsfiles.

rule.statement: Define reusable functions in vars/ (global variables) for use in declarative pipelines. Use src/ classes only for complex stateful logic.
rule.rationale: vars/ functions are callable directly from declarative pipeline steps. src/ classes require script block and import statements, adding friction.

example.code: jenkins-shared-library/ ├── vars/ │ ├── buildApp.groovy # Docker build + push │ ├── deployToK8s.groovy # kubectl set image + rollout status │ └── notifySlack.groovy # Slack notification ├── src/com/example/ │ ├── Docker.groovy # Complex Docker operations class │ └── Kubernetes.groovy # Complex K8s operations class └── resources/ └── templates/ └── deployment.yaml

rule.statement: Shared library functions must accept a Map config parameter with named keys. Fail fast on missing required keys with error().
rule.rationale: Named map parameters are self-documenting and extensible without breaking existing callers. Explicit error() on missing required keys gives clear messages.

rule.statement: Pin shared library version in Jenkinsfile with @Library('my-library@v1.2.3'). Never use @main or @HEAD in production.
rule.rationale: Unpinned library versions mean a library update breaks all consuming pipelines simultaneously without a planned upgrade.

antipattern.description: Writing the same Docker build/push logic in every Jenkinsfile.
antipattern.reason: Copy-paste logic diverges over time. When the registry URL or build flags change, each Jenkinsfile must be updated individually.
